channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Middle East latest: 45 patients and wounded people evacuated from Gaza for treatment, officials say
item.description: Palestinian health authorities say 45 patients and wounded people have been evacuated for treatment outside the war-ravaged Gaza Strip. They left the European Hospital in the southern city of Khan Younis early Tuesday and traveled through the Kerem Shalom Crossing into Israel.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: South Korea to inspect Boeing aircraft as it struggles to find cause of plane crash that killed 179
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean officials said Monday they will conduct safety inspections of all Boeing 737-800 aircraft operated by the country's airlines, as they struggle to determine what caused a plane crash that killed 179 people a day earlier.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Cities across the globe prepare to welcome the New Year in celebrations tinged with local traditions
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — Cities around the world are readying to ring in the New Year with celebrations highlighting local cultures and traditions, after a year roiled by ongoing conflict and political instability.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: AP WAS THERE: Carter announces Middle East peace agreement
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — ___ EDITORS: As part of its coverage of the death of former President Jimmy Carter, The Associated Press is looking back at major events in his life. Carter's success at Camp David negotiating a Mideast Peace agreement between Egypt and Israel was one of his crowning achievements.
item.dc:creator: BARRY SCHWEID, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: AP WAS THERE: Carter announces Middle East peace agreement
item.description: One of Jimmy Carter's crowning achievements as president was the Camp David talks that would deliver Israel's first peace agreement with an Arab nation and make Nobel Peace Prize winners of Egyptian President Anwar Sadat and Israeli Prime Minister Menachem Begin.
item.dc:creator: Barry Schweid, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Politician among protesters arrested in Kenya while calling for an end to alleged abductions
item.description: NAIROBI, Kenya (AP) — An opposition politician and several other protesters were arrested in Kenya on Monday during street demonstrations calling for an end to alleged abductions, that recently targeted young government critics.
item.dc:creator: EVELYNE MUSAMBI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Six shot in 'brazen and heartless attack' in the Bronx: NYPD
item.description: THE BRONX, N.Y. (PIX11) -- Six people, including a 12-year-old girl, were shot Monday afternoon in the Bronx, according to the NYPD. The incident happened around 4:50 p.m. at 3544 White Plains Road, police said.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: How to protect your pets from bird flu
item.description: Veterinarians across the nation are sounding the alarm about increasing bird flu infections in our feline friends.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Jimmy Carter made eradicating Guinea worm disease a top mission
item.description: JARWENG, South Sudan (AP) — Nobel Prize-winning peacemaker Jimmy Carter spent nearly four decades waging war to eliminate an ancient parasite plaguing the world’s poorest people.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM and SAM MEDNICK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Migrant crisis added to record-level US homelessness: HUD
item.description: Sanctuary cities saw homelessness rates jump thanks in part to a surge of migrants being sent from the southern border.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, KRON4

item.title: Judge denies sentencing delay for ex-Sen. Bob Menendez but puts his wife's trial off until February
item.description: A judge says former New Jersey Sen. Bob Menendez will be sentenced as scheduled at the end of January, but he's postponed the start of the Democrat's wife's trial until February.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Move over, Gen Alpha: 2025 marks the start of the Gen Beta era
item.description: If you just found out about Generation Alpha, brace yourself for a new crop of kids set to debut on Jan. 1.
item.dc:creator: Sakshi Venkatraman, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Southerners are cleaning up from tornadoes that swept the region
item.description: Cleanup is underway after a strong storm system spawned hail, rain, high winds and tornadoes across the southern U.S. over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Jamie Stengle And Lekan Oyekanmi, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Northern lights could be visible in upper fringes of the US this New Year's Eve
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — There's a chance solar storms may bring northern lights to several northern U.S states just in time for the new year. The sun expelled two bursts of plasma that are hurtling toward Earth and are expected to arrive early this week, according to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ADITHI RAMAKRISHNAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Census Bureau releases 'population clock' projection for New Year's Day
item.description: Based on current estimates, there will also be one birth every 9 seconds and one death every 9.4 seconds in January 2025.
item.dc:creator: Wil Day, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Northern lights could be visible in upper fringes of the US on New Year's Eve
item.description: There's a chance solar storms may bring northern lights to several northern U.S states just in time for the new year.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: California ‘micronation’ offering reward after fathers, children allegedly burglarize, vandalize property
item.description: The “micronation” of Slowjamastan, located in Southern California, is offering a $1,000 cash reward to anyone who can help bring to justice a group of fathers and their children who allegedly burglarized and vandalized their property.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KTLA 5

item.title: The year in review: Influential people who died in 2024
item.description: It was a life that took him from peanut farming to the presidency. While former President Jimmy Carter ’s time in the White House only lasted one term, the decades afterward were defined by humanitarian work that affected people all around the world.
item.dc:creator: BERNARD McGHEE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Jimmy Carter and Playboy: How ‘the weirdo factor’ rocked ’76
item.description: PLAINS, Ga. (AP) — Jimmy Carter already had drawn months of media scrutiny as a devout Southern Baptist running for president. Then the 1976 Democratic nominee brought up sex and sin as he explained his religious faith to Playboy magazine. Carter was not misquoted.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How to watch WWE's 'Raw' on Netflix: What to know before the Jan. 6 debut
item.description: After 21 years, WWE's "RAW" is leaving cable to join Netflix every Monday night beginning Jan. 6, meaning streaming is the only way to watch the longest-running weekly episodic show.
item.dc:creator: Bill Shannon, KRON4

item.title: UnitedHealthcare taught us ways to deny claims: Former employee
item.description: Collins described crying at her desk while handling calls from desperate patients, as supervisors laughed.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Argentine judge charges 5 people over death of former One Direction star Liam Payne
item.description: BUENOS AIRES, Argentina (AP) — An Argentine judge has confirmed charges against five people in connection with the death of Liam Payne, a former member of musical group One Direction. The judge has ordered preventive prison for two of them for having supplied Payne with drugs.
item.dc:creator: ALMUDENA CALATRAVA Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Washington inmate accused of molesting cellmate after changing gender, transfer to women's prison
item.description: A former Washington inmate is suing the state and prison officials after she says they forced her into a cell with a biological male who molested her.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ford Museum will open display honoring Jimmy Carter's life
item.description: After the death of former President Jimmy Carter, the Gerald R. Ford Museum in Grand Rapids is opening a new display to commemorate his life.
item.dc:creator: Anna Skog, NewsNation

item.title: Trump gives complete and total endorsement of Johnson as Speaker
item.description: President-elect Trump offered his complete and total endorsement of Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) on Monday, days before a House vote on Friday to elect a new Speaker. "Speaker Mike Johnson is a good, hard working, religious man. He will do the right thing, and we will continue to WIN.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Appeals court upholds verdict finding Trump liable for sexually assaulting E. Jean Carroll
item.description: A federal appeals court panel on Monday upheld a jury’s verdict finding President-elect Donald Trump liable for sexually assaulting advice columnist E. Jean Carroll and ordering him to pay $5 million.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Thompson-Herro fight leads to ejections of multiple players and coaches in Heat's victory in Houston
item.description: Rockets forward Amen Thompson threw Heat guard Tyler Herro to the floor to trigger an altercation that resulted in six ejections in the closing minute of Miami's 104-100 victory over Houston on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: US children are struggling with reading
item.description: Learning to read can become exponentially more difficult for older students and adults who have fallen behind, creating grave concerns as the number of individuals struggling with literacy into high school and adulthood is increasing.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KRON4

item.title: These songs dominated the Billboard charts longest
item.description: Every now and then, a new song releases and dominates radio airwaves, Spotify playlists, and, of course, the esteemed Billboard Hot 100 chart—and in terms of those types of hits, 2024 has been pretty impressive.
item.dc:creator: Angela Underwood, Stacker, KRON4

item.title: Olivia Hussey, star of the 1968 film 'Romeo and Juliet,' dies at 73
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Olivia Hussey, the actor who starred as a teenage Juliet in the 1968 film “Romeo and Juliet,” has died, her family said on social media Saturday. She was 73. Hussey died on Friday, “peacefully at home surrounded by her loved ones,” a statement posted to her Instagram account said.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIA HUI, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: North Carolina McDonald's employee fatally shoots DoorDash driver, police say
item.description: A McDonald's employee who was on the loose after a shooting that killed a DoorDash delivery driver Thursday night has been taken into custody.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Sockol, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Sportscasting icon Greg Gumbel dies at 78
item.description: Sportscaster Greg Gumbel, best known for his long career with CBS Sports, has died Friday, Dec. 27, according to CBS Sports and Gumbel's family.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, Fox 5

item.title: Explosion at Tyson Foods plant leaves 1 dead, others injured
item.description: An explosion at a Tyson Foods plant in Georgia late Thursday night left one person dead and others injured.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, KRON4

item.title: Man indicted in burning death of woman inside a New York City subway train, prosecutors say
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The man accused of burning a sleeping woman to death inside a New York City subway train has been indicted on murder and arson charges, prosecutors announced Friday, as authorities continue working to identify the victim.
item.dc:creator: CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Republicans promise to end remote work for federal employees
item.description: When Republicans take control of Washington in January, they say it's back to the office for federal workers.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Biertempfel, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pennsylvania teacher jailed in Russia formally designated as wrongfully detained
item.description: An American schoolteacher arrested in Russia on drug charges more than four years ago has been designated by the U.S. government as wrongfully detained, the State Department said Friday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Bird flu virus in Louisiana patient likely mutated to be more transmissible, CDC says
item.description: The agency, in its latest update released Thursday, said the mutations were not found in samples taken from wild birds, suggesting the virus changed over the course of the patient’s infection.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, Fox 5
